title: YouTube is testing an AI search mode that 'feels more like a conversation'
description: A new feature called Ask YouTube will let you pose complex questions and receive 'comprehensive results that include video and text.'

title: The G512 X is Logitech’s most advanced and customizable gaming keyboard yet
description: Featuring support for both mechanical and analog switch with TMR sensors, rapid trigger tech and an 8K polling rate, the Logitech G512 X might be Logitech's fanciest and most customizable gaming keyboard to date.

title: Images of Samsung's rumored smart glasses have leaked
description: Samsung is reportedly preparing to launch a pair of smart glasses currently codenamed "Jinju." The company is also making a more advanced model with a display.

title: Joby Aviation is demoing 10-minute air taxi flights from JFK to Manhattan for a week
description: The air taxi service is expected to launch in New York, along with Texas and Florida, later this year, pending final FAA approvals.

title: Star Trek: Strange New Worlds returns for its penultimate season on July 23
description: Star Trek: Strange New Worlds returns for its fourth season on July 23. The series has been renewed for a fifth and final season.

title: Valve's Steam Controller costs $99 and arrives May 4
description: Now, we wait for the Steam Machine.

title: Valve Steam Controller review: A gamepad in search of a console
description: The Steam Controller is a sturdy and sleek gamepad that stands up to the competition. It’s for Valve diehards, trackpad fanatics and anyone whose main gaming hub is Steam.

title: A Star Wars expansion is coming to PowerWash Simulator 2
description: So that's what the Empire means by "Rebel scum."

title: OpenAI breaks out of exclusivity agreements in its partnership with Microsoft
description: OpenAI can license its latest AI models to other companies and use other cloud providers to ship its products.

title: Spotify is now a fitness app too
description: The fitness genre will feature on-demand Peloton classes for Premium subscribers and workout playlists for all users.

title: Oprah brings her podcast to Amazon's streaming services
description: Oprah’s Book Club and the back catalog of her long-running TV show are part of the licensing deal too.

title: The sequel to the iconic emulator ZSNES is called Super ZSNES, of course
description: Somehow, ZSNES has returned.

title: Ford's Mustang Cobra Jet sets a new EV quarter mile record at 6.87 seconds
description: Ford Racing's Mustang Cobra Jet 2200 just ran a quarter mile in 6.87 seconds at 221 mph at an NHRA event in Charlotte.

title: Forced Windows updates can now be paused forever
description: You can now continually put a pause Windows 11 updates, 35 days at a time.

title: Canadian premier wants to ban social media and AI chatbots for kids in Manitoba
description: Wab Kinew's proposal would only affect Manitoba while other efforts are being pursued that would adopt a ban across Canada.

title: Trump has terminated several members of the independent National Science Board
description: The Trump administration sent letters to several members of the National Science Board, informing them that their positions were "terminated, effective immediately."

title: BYD's next all-electric hypercar is a convertible that's coming to Europe first
description: The Denza Z currently boasts more than 1,000 horsepower and a 0 to 60 mph in less than two seconds.

title: OpenAI's Sam Altman apologizes for not reporting ChatGPT account of Tumbler Ridge suspect to police
description: The CEO's letter promised to "find ways to prevent tragedies like this in the future" by working with law enforcement and all levels of government.

title: NASA's initial takeaways from the Artemis II mission, and more science stories
description: NASA has begun its initial assessments of the Orion spacecraft, SLS rocket and other systems that brought astronauts to the moon.

title: What to read this weekend: Monsters in the Archives dives deep into Stephen King's early works
description: This week, we read Monsters in the Archives: My Year of Fear with Stephen King and the first issue of the Image Comics miniseries, Corpse Knight.

title: Tesla is giving away one year free Supercharging with Model 3 Premium and Performance purchases
description: Tesla has announced that it’s including one year of free supercharging with a Model 3 Premium or Performance vehicle.

title: Engadget review recap: DJI Osmo Pocket 4, Recteq X-Fire Pro and Alienware 27 QD-OLED
description: A roundup of the latest reviews from Engadget.

title: Vampire Crawlers, Peter Molyneux's return and other new indie games worth checking out
description: Plus, a photography-based heists and a slice-of-life soccer game.

title: XChat, the standalone app for messaging on X, is available on iOS now
description: X has launched XChat, a standalone app for accessing the social platform's messaging feature.

title: Maine governor vetoes bill temporarily banning large data centers in the state
description: Maine is not moving forward with a moratorium on AI data center construction until fall 2027.

title: A Battlefield movie adaptation is on the way, possibly starring Michael B. Jordan
description: Mission Impossible honcho Christopher McQuarrie is set to write, direct and produce.

title: The DOJ is backing xAI in its lawsuit against Colorado
description: The DOJ is joining xAI's lawsuit against Colorado and claims that the state's recent AI law is unconstitutional.

title: What you need to know as Elon Musk's lawsuit against Sam Altman begins
description: In a few short days, jury selection will begin in the long-awaited Musk v. Altman case. At the end of that process, an Oakland federal court will task nine regular people with deciding if OpenAI defrauded Elon Musk.

title: Google plans to invest even more money into Anthropic
description: Google is investing $10 billion in Anthropic, and could add an additional $30 billion if the AI startup hits certain milestones.

title: Singapore police arrest alleged The Legend of Aang: The Last Airbender leaker
description: "I saw it's just a Paramount+ thing, so I decided I'd troll a little bit."

title: The MacBook Neo is a glimpse into John Ternus's Apple
description: John Ternus was unavoidable when Apple debuted the Macbook Neo.

title: Engadget Podcast: Tim Cook’s Apple era and what lies ahead for John Ternus
description: Tim Cook will soon be stepping down as Apple's CEO, in this episode we discuss his legacy and what new CEO John Ternus could bring.

title: DeepSeek promises its new AI model has 'world-class' reasoning
description: DeepSeek has released its latest AI models, the V4 Pro and Flash versions.

title: The Morning After: Polymarket and a hairdryer
description: The biggest news stories this morning:  Here’s to the stable ones: In praise of Tim Cook, Meta is downsizing by about 10 percent, Hey Meta workers, are you getting paid for those keystrokes?

title: Porsche's new Cayenne Turbo Coupé Electric can do 0-60 mph in 2.5 seconds
description: Porsche has announced an electric version of its popular Cayenne Coupe that looks to be the company's most powerful vehicle ever — either ICE or electric.

title: US soldier arrested for allegedly making over $400,000 on Polymarket with classified Maduro information
description: US soldier Gannon Ken Van Dyke has been arrested and charged for placing bets on prediction marketplace Polymarket using classified information he had access to related to the capture of former Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro.

title: Claude can now connect to lifestyle apps like Spotify, Instacart and AllTrails
description: Anthropic is expanding its directory of connected services for its Claude AI chatbot.

title: Microsoft is reportedly offering voluntary buyouts to up to 7 percent of its employees
description: Microsoft reportedly plans to offer voluntary buyouts to up to 7 percent of its US employees in May as a way of avoiding layoffs.

title: Meta is downsizing by about 10 percent
description: Meta is making another steep cut to its staff, this time to the tune of a 10 percent reduction in its workforce.

title: Titanium Court mashes together genres and cultural references to tell a strange, funny tale
description: AP Thomson's award winner is one of the most inventive, captivating games of 2026.

title: X is shutting down its Communities feature
description: X is directing Communities users to move to group chats in its XChat app before the feature is retired at the end of May.

title: Apple, Amazon join push for looser greenhouse emissions reporting
description: The Greenhouse Gas Protocol is considering changes to how certain types of emissions are reported, but some companies are arguing against the proposed rules.

title: Apple TV's upcoming For All Mankind spinoff Star City oozes Cold War-era paranoia
description: Apple TV just dropped a real trailer for Star City, the streamer's spinoff of For All Mankind. This one takes a look at the Soviet space program.

title: Rivian begins production on the R2 electric SUV
description: If you're eyeing the $45,000 base model, you'll have to wait around 18 months.

title: Five Annapurna Interactive games get Switch 2 releases
description: Free upgrades for Sayonara Wild Hearts and Lorelei and the Laser Eyes are available now.

title: Ubisoft has finally dropped a trailer for that Assassin's Creed: Black Flag remake
description: Ubisoft has finally given us details and a short trailer for Assassin's Creed: Black Flag Resynced. This is a remake of the 2013 original.

title: Someone allegedly used a hairdryer to rig Polymarket weather bets
description: French authorities are investigating a case in which a hairdryer was allegedly used to change weather sensor data. This was reportedly used to rig bets on Polymarket.

title: Turkey wants to ban social media for kids under 15
description: A bill has been passed and now needs approval from the country’s President to become law.

title: Meta will show parents the topics of their teens' AI conversations
description: With countries banning social media for kids left and right, Meta is trying different things to convince parents that its platforms are safe for teens.

title: 2027 BMW i7 first look: A fantastically techy car for the 1 percent
description: If you like tech and gadgets, the new BMW i7 packs practically every futuristic feature and gizmo you could ask for, along with some you may not even know you wanted.
